verses.text: David pours out love to the LORD his strength, his rock, fortress, and deliverer, on whom he calls and is saved. He heaps up names for his God. The believer who has been rescued cannot find words enough for the One who saved him.

David pours out love to the LORD his strength, his rock, fortress, and deliverer, on whom he calls and is saved. He heaps up names for his God. The believer who has been rescued cannot find words enough for the One who saved him.

David pours out love to the LORD his strength, his rock, fortress, and deliverer, on whom he calls and is saved. He heaps up names for his God. The believer who has been rescued cannot find words enough for the One who saved him.

The cords of death once entangled him, but in his distress he called, and God heard his voice from His temple. Prayer reached heaven from the deepest trouble. No pit is so deep that the cry of faith cannot rise from it to God.

The cords of death once entangled him, but in his distress he called, and God heard his voice from His temple. Prayer reached heaven from the deepest trouble. No pit is so deep that the cry of faith cannot rise from it to God.

The cords of death once entangled him, but in his distress he called, and God heard his voice from His temple. Prayer reached heaven from the deepest trouble. No pit is so deep that the cry of faith cannot rise from it to God.

David pictures God coming to his rescue with shaking earth, smoke, and fire, riding upon a cherub and flying on the wings of the wind. Heaven itself is moved for him. The God who governs the storm bends all His power to save His own.

Amid hail and thunder the LORD's rebuke lays bare the very foundations of the world. Creation trembles at His voice. The forces that terrify men are the servants God wields against His enemies.

He reached down from on high, drew David out of deep waters, and set him in a large place, 'because he delighted in me.' Rescue flowed from God's love. We are saved not by our worth but by the delight of His grace.

He reached down from on high, drew David out of deep waters, and set him in a large place, 'because he delighted in me.' Rescue flowed from God's love. We are saved not by our worth but by the delight of His grace.

David testifies that God rewarded his righteousness and clean hands, for he kept the ways of the LORD. He speaks of sincere, not sinless, obedience. God honours the integrity of a heart that truly seeks to walk with Him.

With the merciful God shows mercy, with the pure He shows Himself pure, and the perverse He meets in kind; He saves the afflicted but brings down proud looks. God deals with men according to their hearts. The humble find mercy; the haughty meet resistance.

With the merciful God shows mercy, with the pure He shows Himself pure, and the perverse He meets in kind; He saves the afflicted but brings down proud looks. God deals with men according to their hearts. The humble find mercy; the haughty meet resistance.

God lights David's lamp and turns his darkness to day; by Him he runs through a troop and leaps a wall, for 'the way of God is perfect.' Strength for every obstacle comes from the Lord. His tried word is a shield to all who trust Him.

God lights David's lamp and turns his darkness to day; by Him he runs through a troop and leaps a wall, for 'the way of God is perfect.' Strength for every obstacle comes from the Lord. His tried word is a shield to all who trust Him.

Who is God but the LORD, the rock who girds David with strength, makes his feet sure as a deer's, and trains his hands for battle? All ability comes from God. The skill and strength we use are gifts to be traced back to Him.

Who is God but the LORD, the rock who girds David with strength, makes his feet sure as a deer's, and trains his hands for battle? All ability comes from God. The skill and strength we use are gifts to be traced back to Him.

God gave him the shield of salvation, held him up, and enlarged his steps so he did not slip, subduing his enemies under him. Victory is God's doing. The believer's standing and triumph rest on the upholding hand of God.

God gave him the shield of salvation, held him up, and enlarged his steps so he did not slip, subduing his enemies under him. Victory is God's doing. The believer's standing and triumph rest on the upholding hand of God.
His foes were put to flight and beaten as small as dust before the wind. The enemy was utterly overthrown. When God fights for His people, no power arrayed against them can endure.
His foes were put to flight and beaten as small as dust before the wind. The enemy was utterly overthrown. When God fights for His people, no power arrayed against them can endure.

God set David over the nations, and strangers submitted and came trembling out of their strongholds. His kingdom was widened. This foreshadows Christ, before whom the nations will at last bow.

'The LORD liveth; and blessed be my rock,' cries David, for it is God who avenges him and subdues his foes. Praise rises from rescue. The living God is the unfailing rock on whom His people build.
'The LORD liveth; and blessed be my rock,' cries David, for it is God who avenges him and subdues his foes. Praise rises from rescue. The living God is the unfailing rock on whom His people build.
'The LORD liveth; and blessed be my rock,' cries David, for it is God who avenges him and subdues his foes. Praise rises from rescue. The living God is the unfailing rock on whom His people build.

Therefore David will give thanks among the nations, for God grants great deliverance to His king and mercy 'to David, and to his seed for evermore.' The promise reaches beyond David. It rests at last on Christ, his Son and Lord, and all His seed.
